---
title: "Project Title Here"
description: "One-line summary of what this project does and why."
author:
  - name:
      given: Diego Filippo
      family: Marino
date: "2026-01-01"
categories:
  - Python
  - Machine Learning
# image: thumbnail.png
# bibliography: references.bib
# jupyter: python3
draft: true
---

## Overview

What this project is, what problem it solves, and why it exists. One to three paragraphs.

Link to the full repository: [GitHub](https://github.com/DiegoFilippoMarino/repo-name).

## Motivation

Why this project was built. What gap it fills. What paper or idea it implements.

If implementing a paper, cite it:

> Author et al., *"Paper Title"*, Conference/Journal, Year. [arXiv:XXXX.XXXXX](https://arxiv.org/abs/XXXX.XXXXX)

## Architecture

Describe the system design, model architecture, or algorithmic approach.

$$\hat{y} = f_\theta(x) = \sigma(W_2 \cdot \text{ReLU}(W_1 x + b_1) + b_2)$$

### Key components

- **Component A** — what it does
- **Component B** — what it does
- **Component C** — what it does

## Implementation

### Setup

``` bash
git clone https://github.com/DiegoFilippoMarino/repo-name.git
cd repo-name
uv sync
```

### Usage

``` python
from project import Model

model = Model(config)
model.train(dataset)
results = model.evaluate(test_set)
```

### Configuration

| Parameter       | Default | Description               |
|:----------------|:-------:|:--------------------------|
| `learning_rate` |  3e-4   | Optimizer learning rate   |
| `batch_size`    |   64    | Training batch size       |
| `epochs`        |   100   | Number of training epochs |
| `hidden_dim`    |   256   | Hidden layer dimension    |

: Default configuration parameters.

## Results

Summarize experimental results. Include plots, tables, or comparisons.

```{{python}}
#| label: fig-results
#| fig-cap: "Training loss over epochs."

import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

epochs = np.arange(1, 101)
loss = 2.0 * np.exp(-0.03 * epochs) + 0.1 * np.random.randn(100) * np.exp(-0.02 * epochs)

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(7, 3))
ax.plot(epochs, loss, color="#2a6e6e", linewidth=1.2, alpha=0.8)
ax.set_xlabel("Epoch")
ax.set_ylabel("Loss")
ax.spines[["top", "right"]].set_visible(False)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

::: callout-note
## Reproducibility

All experiments use seed `42`. Full training logs are available in the repository.
:::

## Limitations

What this project does not do. Known issues. Scope boundaries.

## Future work

- Planned improvement 1
- Planned improvement 2
- Planned improvement 3

## References

::: {#refs}
:::
